bat.intent: Pretty print the contents of one or more files to `stdout`
Concatenate several files into the target file

btm.intent: Show the default layout (CPU, memory, temperatures, disk, network, and processes)
Enable basic mode, removing charts and condensing data (similar to `top`)
Use big dots instead of small ones in charts

choose.intent: Print the 5th item from a line (starting from 0)
Print the first, 3rd, and 5th item from a line, where items are separated by ':' instead of whitespace
Print everything from the 2nd to 5th item on the line, including the 5th

delta.intent: Compare files or directories, showing the line numbers
Compare files or directories, showing the differences side by side

duf.intent: List everything (such as pseudo, duplicate, or inaccessible filesystems)
Only show specified devices or mount points

dust.intent: Display information for the current directory
Display information about one or more directories

entr.intent: Rebuild with `make` if any file in any subdirectory changes
Rebuild and test with `make` if any `.c` source files in the current directory change
Send a `SIGTERM` to any previously spawned ruby subprocesses before executing `ruby main.rb`

exa.intent: List all files, including hidden files
Long format list (permissions, ownership, size, and modification date) of all files

eza.intent: List all files, including hidden files
Long format list (permissions, ownership, size, and modification date) of all files

fd.intent: Recursively find files matching a specific pattern in the current directory
Find files in a specific directory
Find files with a specific extension

fzf.intent: Start `fzf` on all files in the specified directory
Select multiple files with `<Shift Tab>` and write to a file

hexyl.intent: Print the hexadecimal representation of a file
Print the hexadecimal representation of the first n bytes of a file

hyperfine.intent: Run a basic benchmark, performing at least 10 runs
Change minimum number of benchmarking runs

jq.intent: Execute a specific expression only using the `jq` binary (print a colored and formatted JSON output)

mlr.intent: Pretty-print a CSV file in a tabular format
Receive JSON data and pretty print the output

parallel.intent: Gzip several files at once, using all cores
Read arguments from `stdin`, run 4 jobs at once
Convert JPEG images to PNG using replacement strings

procs.intent: List all processes showing the PID, user, CPU usage, memory usage, and the command which started them
List all processes as a tree
List information about processes, if the commands which started them contain Zsh

pv.intent: Print the contents of the file and display a progress bar
Measure the speed and amount of data flow between pipes (`--size` is optional)
Filter a file, see both progress and amount of output data

rg.intent: Recursively search current directory for a pattern (`regex`)
Recursively search for a pattern in a file or directory
Search for a literal string pattern

sd.intent: Trim some whitespace using a `regex` (output stream: `stdout`)
Replace words using capture groups (output stream: `stdout`)
Find and replace in a specific file (output stream: `stdout`)

sponge.intent: Append file content to the source file
Remove all lines starting with # in a file

watchexec.intent: Call `ls -la` when any file in the current directory changes
Run `make` when any JavaScript, CSS, and HTML file in the current directory changes
Run `make` when any file in the `lib` or `src` directory changes

xsv.intent: Inspect the headers of a file
Get an overview of the shape of entries

yq.intent: Output a YAML file, in pretty-print format (v4+)
Output a YAML file, in pretty-print format (v3)
Output the first element in a YAML file that contains only an array (v4+)
